_comment: CHAD Margin/Buying-Power BLOCK — pre-registered config per design SSOT v2.2. Committed BEFORE the consuming gate exists (pre-registration). THRESHOLDS ARE FROZEN: changing any threshold/rate is a deliberate logged event, and a post-FAIL change counts as a new trial. The SOLE field expected to change post-freeze is `mode` (the Part 7 shadow->enforce ladder); each mode change is a separate authorization commit, so git history is the enforcement-authorization log. No env overrides; invalid/missing config => refuse to start (fail-closed at startup).
_mode_note: shadow = evaluate + log only, blocks nothing (design Part 7 step 2). enforce_paper / enforce_live require a separate deliberate authorization commit; never auto-flip. This is the only field permitted to change after freeze.
_basis_note: ALL percentage/multiple thresholds are denominated in NetLiquidation (the canonical account-size field the buying_power_provider fetches). The design's word 'equity' maps to NetLiquidation here; EquityWithLoanValue is NOT collected and is not a basis. One base, no ambiguity.

margin_rates_by_asset_class._note: Independent margin estimate = notional * rate, then CAD-converted. Conservative (overstates margin => biases toward BLOCK). The MORE CONSERVATIVE of {whatIf, independent} governs.
margin_rates_by_asset_class._futures_note: Single conservative blanket rate. Futures are gated OFF (CHAD_DISABLE_FUTURES_EXECUTION=1). 0.25 deliberately overstates typical SPAN (~5-12%) to bias toward BLOCK. MUST be refined to per-symbol SPAN rates before futures execution is ever enabled.

freshness._fx_note: USD->CAD via canonical USDCAD_CONVERSION_CONSTANT * this bias, overstating USD exposure in CAD (FX fails-closed). Applies to exposure, NOT to capacity figures (design section 2.1).

whatif_pacing._note: Respect IBKR pacing. On pacing/timeout the independent estimate governs; an increasing order is still fail-closed if neither is computable.

safety._note: Loader contract (implemented by the design-Phase-B gate that consumes this file): STRICT validation. Unknown non-underscore key => refuse to start; missing required key => refuse; wrong type => refuse. A typo'd threshold name must NOT validate as a harmless extra key while the real threshold silently falls to a code default — that silent drift is exactly what this file exists to prevent. A risk threshold must not be silently overridable by env/drop-in (loss-guard drift lesson).
